documentation.readme: A suite of WiFi/Bluetooth offensive and defensive tools for the ESP32
usage_sections: Getting Started: Download the latest release of the firmware. Check out the project wiki for a full overview of the ESP32 Marauder

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --version
arguments.description: Print the tool version and exit.

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

arguments.name: --force
arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with justcallmekoko/ESP32-Deauther (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with justcallmekoko/ArduinoPcap (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with zynon/LdapRelayScan (score=9); pair in the chain planner.
Phase 3: same attack_surface=['web'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.
Phase 4: same attack_surface=['web'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.
Phase 5: same attack_surface=['web'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the wifi category. Fits when the engagement needs: A suite of WiFi/Bluetooth offensive and defensive tools for the ESP32.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: A suite of WiFi/Bluetooth offensive and defensive tools for the ESP32.
documentation.how_to_use: Clone or install `ESP32Marauder` from its upstream repository, then run `ESP32Marauder --help` (or the README entrypoint) to confirm flags. A suite of WiFi/Bluetooth offensive and defensive tools for the ESP32.

name: ESP32Marauder
summary: A suite of WiFi/Bluetooth offensive and defensive tools for the ESP32

trust.warning: Attribution / index entry only. The operator must audit the code, the README, the license, and the releases before invoking. Phase 5 — entry is auto-generated from the operator's curated fetch list; no automated enrichment yet.

use_cases: When you need wifi, use ESP32Marauder because A suite of WiFi/Bluetooth offensive and defensive tools for the ESP32.
When chaining wifi tools, pair ESP32Marauder with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `ESP32Marauder --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
